Name: pac_bayes_jacobian_layer_skipping
Title: Enforcing Latency Budgets via PAC-Bayes Layer Skipping in Neural Networks
Short Hypothesis: By utilizing PAC-Bayes principles and Jacobian spectral analysis, we can create a layer-skipping mechanism that optimally selects layers to skip during inference, ensuring strict adherence to latency budgets without compromising accuracy.
Related Work: Existing works have explored PAC-Bayes bounds and layer skipping in neural networks, but none have integrated these concepts to create a latency-aware inference mechanism. This proposal distinguishes itself by explicitly linking Jacobian spectral analysis with PAC-Bayes frameworks to enforce strict latency constraints.
Abstract: In this proposal, we investigate a novel approach to machine learning inference that strictly adheres to latency budgets while maintaining model accuracy. By leveraging PAC-Bayes principles, we derive a layer-skipping mechanism that utilizes Jacobian spectral analysis to determine which layers can be skipped during inference. This mechanism ensures that neural networks can operate efficiently in latency-critical applications, such as autonomous driving and real-time video analysis. We will empirically validate this approach through a series of experiments that assess the trade-offs between accuracy and latency, ultimately contributing to a more efficient deployment of machine learning models in resource-constrained environments. Our proposed framework aims to bridge the gap between theoretical foundations and practical applications, providing a robust solution for latency-aware inference.
Experiments: We will conduct experiments on standard neural network architectures (e.g., ResNet, VGG) to evaluate the proposed layer-skipping mechanism. The experiments will include: 1. Baseline performance measurement of the model without skipping layers. 2. Implementation of the PAC-Bayes layer-skipping mechanism and performance measurement. 3. Comparative analysis of accuracy and latency under varying latency budgets. Metrics: inference accuracy, average latency, and throughput will be used to evaluate performance.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risks include the challenge of accurately estimating the Jacobian spectra in real-time, which could lead to suboptimal layer selection. Additionally, the model's performance may degrade if the selected layers are critical for certain inference tasks. Limitations also arise from the need for extensive empirical validation across various model architectures and datasets.
